mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month growth in retail sales signals near-term demand resilience and stronger revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the sales trend is rising.
evidence_refs: series:RSAFS
fact:latest RSAFS snapshot available at t-1mo was 453438.0
failure_condition: Reject if rising 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than the unconditional hit rate over a 3-year rolling walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A crossing into a faster federal funds tightening path raises equity discount rates and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days after the policy-rate acceleration is observed.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by at least 0.20pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
fact:latest FEDFUNDS snapshot available at t-1mo was 0.37
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or the triggered mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth deceleration combined with a rising unemployment rate signals weakening household income and recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change falls below its 12-month average while UNRATE rises at least 0.2pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: series:PAYEMS
series:UNRATE
fact:latest PAYEMS snapshot available at t-1mo was 143915.0
fact:latest UNRATE snapshot available at t-1mo was 5.0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor weakening trigger has nonnegative average 60-day forward returns or if either proxy alone fully explains the signal with no incremental interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production signals weakening goods-sector output and lower operating leverage for cyclical firms, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO
fact:latest INDPRO snapshot available at t-2mo was 103.3759
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO contraction triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or the downside return differential is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer prices while the federal funds rate remains below 1% signals policy falling behind inflation, raising future tightening risk and reducing equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation crosses above 2.5% while FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%
evidence_refs: series:CPIAUCSL
series:FEDFUNDS
fact:latest CPIAUCSL snapshot available at t-2mo was 237.92
fact:latest FEDFUNDS snapshot available at t-1mo was 0.37
failure_condition: Reject if the joint inflation-low-rate trigger fails to predict lower 20-day returns than periods with only high CPI inflation or only low FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in housing starts above its recent trend signals stronger construction activity, credit creation, and durable-goods demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above its 6-month moving average
evidence_refs: series:HOUST
fact:latest HOUST snapshot available at t-2mo was 1089.0
failure_condition: Reject if upward HOUST trend-crossing observations do not have higher average 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered observations in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A falling unemployment-rate trend signals improving labor-market slack and household income prospects, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through short-horizon risk sentiment.
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
fact:latest UNRATE snapshot available at t-1mo was 5.0
failure_condition: Reject if declines in UNRATE over the prior 3 months have a 5-day forward return hit rate no higher than the unconditional hit rate over rolling 2-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: Weak real GDP growth combined with a non-declining policy rate signals a growth scare without immediate monetary offset, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth crosses below 1.0% while FEDFUNDS is flat or rising over 3 months
evidence_refs: series:GDPC1
series:FEDFUNDS
fact:latest GDPC1 snapshot available at t-4mo was 16492.7
fact:latest FEDFUNDS snapshot available at t-1mo was 0.37
failure_condition: Reject if the weak-GDP and non-easing-rate trigger produces 60-day returns that are not below the unconditional mean or if the interaction term is unstable across rolling samples.
